---
title: "TensorToFeatureVector"
---

## Overview

`TensorToFeatureVector` decodes a global feature descriptor from a model output tensor into a
flat `std::vector<float>` for storage in `TensorData`.

## Tensor Layout

The decoder accepts **1D** or **2D** tensors:

| Rank | Layout | Behavior |
|------|--------|----------|
| 1    | `[C]`  | Returns all `C` feature values. `ctx.batch_index` is ignored. |
| 2    | `[B, C]` | Returns the `C`-length vector at `ctx.batch_index`. |

This decoder is typically used after a post-encoder such as `GlobalAvgPoolModule`, which
reduces a spatial `[B, C, H, W]` feature map to `[B, C]`.

## Validation

`decode()` validates at runtime:

- Tensor is defined and at least 1-dimensional
- Tensor rank is 1 or 2 (not 0D, 3D, or higher)
- For 2D inputs, `batch_index` is in `[0, B)`

Invalid inputs throw `std::invalid_argument` or `std::out_of_range` after logging the
failure at `spdlog::debug` level.
